title: Web Data, Fetch, Storage, and Security Boundaries · Independent Script Trial
summary: Load and cache public service data while handling hostile input, offline states, privacy, and response failures. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a resilient privacy-aware service-data client with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use fetch with explicit method, headers, body, status checks, parsing, abort, and failure-state behavior.
Construct and parse URL, URLSearchParams, FormData, and encoded payloads without manual delimiter bugs.
Parse, validate, transform, and serialize JSON while accounting for unsupported values and hostile object shape.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
